message:
Expand AST statement types, parser logical-line merging, module resolver probe tracking

Add 12 new Statement variants (Block, If, For, While, DoWhile, Return,
Throw, TryCatch, Switch, Break, Continue, FunctionDeclaration) across
the AST, canonical-value serialization, and span accessors. This is the
foundation for parsing control-flow constructs beyond import/export/var.

Parser gains a LogicalLine merging layer that tracks brace/paren/bracket
depth and quote state to coalesce multi-physical-line block statements
into single logical units before dispatch. New parse_* helpers cover if,
for, while, do-while, return, throw, try/catch/finally, switch, break,
continue, and function declarations with full parameter lists.

Module resolver now returns a probe_sequence Vec<String> alongside the
resolved specifier and record, recording every candidate path attempted
during resolution. The new lookup_workspace_candidate_with_probes helper
threads probe tracking through relative, absolute, and bare specifier
resolution paths. ResolvedModule gains a probe_sequence field (serde
skip_serializing_if empty).

Static semantics analysis (analyze_statement) gains recursive handlers
for all 12 new statement types, propagating await-in-expression checks,
scope bindings, and lexical/var name tracking through nested blocks.

Parser arena implements VariableDeclaration allocation (previously
unimplemented!()), with per-declarator span allocation, byte charging,
and optional initializer expression lowering. Adds test helpers and
from_syntax_tree round-trip tests for multi-declarator const statements.

~1900 lines of new functionality across 5 source files.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
